title: mullaghera decumbens
side_effects: Mullaghera decumbens can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its toxic compounds. In severe cases, improper use of the plant can lead to kidney damage, liver failure, and even death. Some individuals may also experience allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing, after exposure to the plant's potent sap. Additionally, long-term exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can cause neurological damage, resulting in symptoms such as seizures, tremors, and memory loss.
